hypothesis: The current gate stack covers syntax (php -l), layout regression (structural markers), template breadth (6-URL set), WP runtime errors (body error scan), and PHP 7.4-removed/deprecated patterns (grep gate). The remaining unaddressed failure class is behavioral PHP 8.x changes that are legal in PHP 7.4 but break in PHP 8.x without being catchable by grep: strict null coercion (null passed to non-nullable int/string params now throws TypeError), dynamic property deprecation (PHP 8.2+), `match` keyword conflicts in identifiers, and `str_contains`/`str_starts_with` availability gaps. PHPCompatibility PHPCS standard (`phpcs --standard=PHPCompatibility --runtime-set testVersion 8.1`) statically analyses .php files for all these patterns and produces ERROR/WARNING output per line. Running it on all changed .php files before push will catch behavioral incompatibilities during the SiteGround migration window (deadline May 20, 2026, now 16 days away). Gate: block push on any PHPCompatibility ERROR; allow on WARNING. Implementation requires: `composer global require squizlabs/php_codesniffer && composer global require phpcompatibility/php-compatibility && phpcs --config-set installed_paths ~/.composer/vendor/phpcompatibility/php-compatibility/PHPCompatibility`.
measurement: Percentage of deploys where CI smoke test passes on first push (track via git log + CI run history). Secondary: zero false-positive blocks from PHPCompatibility WARNINGS on clean PHP 8.x code.
changes_description: PENDING LOCAL IMPLEMENTATION — cloud session cannot install phpcs. Once implemented, gate runs on all changed .php files, blocks on ERROR, warns on WARNING. Surface hypothesis section updated now; Current Approach step 4.75 to be added by local agent post-install.
